title: The One Where Emma Cries

jokes.text: l would have said yes to anybody. Like that time when you and l got married.
jokes.setup: Rachel explaining she would have accepted any marriage proposal in her emotional state
jokes.punchline: Casually references her own drunken Vegas marriage as proof of poor judgment
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that uses established continuity to deflate tension. Perfect character voice - Rachel's casual delivery of something mortifying.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I was in such an emotional state, I would have said yes to anyone who proposed to me.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I married Ross in Vegas!

jokes.text: l'm not helping.
jokes.setup: Rachel realizes her Vegas marriage example makes things worse
jokes.punchline: Deadpan acknowledgment of her own poor argument
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and self-awareness. Two words that perfectly capture the moment's awkwardness.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God, I married him in Vegas.

jokes.text: Well, Chandler's my oldest friend. Joey's my-- No!
jokes.setup: Ross trying to clarify the friendship hierarchy between Chandler and Joey
jokes.punchline: Realizes mid-sentence this distinction makes things worse and aborts
jokes.explanation: Solid character moment showing Ross's tendency to overthink and dig himself deeper.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Okay, so Chandler is my best friend, but Joey is like my best friend too, just in a different way—

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: No, wait, that's not... I'm just going to stop talking now.

jokes.text: This isn't the right room. Sorry, folks.
jokes.setup: Joey walks in on tense conversation about Rachel saying she'd marry him
jokes.punchline: Immediately backs out pretending he's in wrong place
jokes.explanation: Standard sitcom awkward entrance gag. Serviceable but not particularly creative.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa! I'm in the wrong place!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I'm going to go... somewhere else. Anywhere else.

jokes.text: No, they ran out of 'lt's a Girl!' But l can fix this one.
jokes.setup: Phoebe explaining why she bought 'It's a Boy' sign
jokes.punchline: Visual of crudely modified sign to change 'Boy' to 'Girl'
jokes.explanation: Typical Phoebe crafty solution. Visual gag works but is fairly predictable.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I bought an 'It's a Boy' sign, but then I found out it was a girl, so I just changed the 'B' to a 'Gr'.

jokes.text: See? Huh? So sexy.
jokes.setup: Phoebe showing off her modified 'It's a Girl' sign
jokes.punchline: Monica's deadpan 'So sexy' response to obviously amateur craft work
jokes.explanation: Monica's dry response to Phoebe's enthusiasm. Good contrast between characters.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: It's a Girl!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: So sexy.

jokes.text: Am l producing them?
jokes.setup: Monica says Ross and Rachel will be back soon
jokes.punchline: Chandler treats them like a TV show he's in charge of scheduling
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler line - meta-theatrical observation delivered with his signature deadpan timing.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Ross and Rachel will be back soon.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Great! Because I've decided they're being moved to Thursday nights at 8.

jokes.text: You know what you should've done? Told yourself that little story.
jokes.setup: Chandler describes boring internal monologue about sleep calculations
jokes.punchline: Monica suggests his own sleep story would have been a cure for insomnia
jokes.explanation: Sharp comeback that plays on how boring Chandler's rambling was. Good spouse banter.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I calculated that if I go to sleep right now, I can get four hours of sleep before I have to wake up.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh, that would've put me right to sleep.

jokes.text: A giant, stuffed gorilla that takes up the entire apartment. What are people think--? You guys, l love it.
jokes.setup: Rachel sees impractically huge stuffed animal gift
jokes.punchline: Starts to complain, then catches herself and forces enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Rachel's people-pleasing instincts overriding practical concerns.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God, this is... this is huge. How am I supposed to... I mean, where would I even... Oh! It's so... cute! Thank you so much!

jokes.text: Hey, what do l know? l wanted to get a bigger gorilla.
jokes.setup: Discussion about whether Joey should leave before Ross arrives
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's non-sequitur about wanting even more impractical gift
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe - missing the point entirely and doubling down on absurdity.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: You know what I want? I want a teepee.

jokes.text: She's just so cute. l just want to bite her ear off and use it as a sucking candy.
jokes.setup: Phoebe expressing affection for baby Emma
jokes.punchline: Disturbing cannibalistic imagery presented as cute sentiment
jokes.explanation: Peak Phoebe - genuinely disturbing imagery delivered with complete innocence. Memorable and quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh, she's so cute, I could just eat her up!

jokes.text: But l could take one of those little feet and put it in a pita pocket.
jokes.setup: Following up her ear-biting comment
jokes.punchline: Even more specific and disturbing baby-eating fantasy
jokes.explanation: Escalates the disturbing baby-eating theme. The specificity of 'pita pocket' makes it funnier and more unsettling.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh, I know! I'd start with the soft parts—the ears and the little baby toes. Maybe work my way up to the sweet, sweet thighs. And then I'd save the head for last because that's where all the delicious brains are!

jokes.text: Wow, you guys got a hospital? Fancy.
jokes.setup: Ross says he left diapers 'in the hospital'
jokes.punchline: Phoebe misinterprets as them owning a hospital
jokes.explanation: Simple misunderstanding joke. Phoebe's literal interpretation works for her character.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: You guys own a hospital?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: No, I left them at the hospital.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh my God, you just abandoned your diapers at the hospital? That's so irresponsible!

jokes.text: Please, take your time. lt's an important decision, not like... say, l don't know, deciding to marry someone. This is about a muffin.
jokes.setup: Rachel taking time to decide what muffin she wants
jokes.punchline: Ross sarcastically contrasts trivial muffin choice with Rachel's impulsive engagement
jokes.explanation: Good passive-aggressive Ross moment. The ironic comparison effectively conveys his lingering resentment.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Yeah, take your time. It's not like you made any hasty decisions lately.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I mean, what's the worst that could happen? You end up with a blueberry instead of chocolate chip?

jokes.text: He got over the 'We were on a break' thing quickly.
jokes.setup: Monica asks how long Ross will stay mad
jokes.punchline: Rachel sarcastically references Ross's multi-season obsession with their break
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback to the show's most famous running gag. Perfect timing and delivery for maximum ironic impact.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: How long do you think he'll stay mad?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh, I don't know. How long were we broken up for?

jokes.text: She told me to ring her if she's not up by 1 1. She has a job interview.
jokes.setup: Rachel asking when baby Emma will wake up
jokes.punchline: Phoebe treats newborn like adult roommate with schedule
jokes.explanation: Great Phoebe absurdism - treating baby like responsible adult. The job interview detail seals it.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: When do you think Emma will wake up?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh, probably around noon. She likes to sleep in. We have an arrangement — I don't bother her before 10, and she doesn't bother me before 10.

jokes.text: Well, l can do whatever l want. l made her.
jokes.setup: Monica warns against waking sleeping baby
jokes.punchline: Rachel asserts maternal authority with creator logic
jokes.explanation: New parent logic taken to extreme. Rachel's possessive 'I made her' has slight dark comedy edge.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Don't wake the baby!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: It's okay, I'm the mother. I made her.

jokes.text: Well, l would say l told you so, but she's kind of doing that for me.
jokes.setup: Baby Emma starts crying after Rachel wakes her
jokes.punchline: Monica notes the baby's crying serves as the 'I told you so'
jokes.explanation: Clever observation that the baby's reaction is the perfect comeback. Well-structured with good restraint.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, there's your 'I told you so'

jokes.text: How long do you think we have to stay?
jokes.setup: Baby crying heartbreakingly while others express sympathy
jokes.punchline: Chandler's blunt question about escape timing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler moment - saying what everyone's thinking while others are being supportive. Great timing.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: So when can we give it back?

jokes.text: You're gonna love Oklahoma.
jokes.setup: Chandler agrees to run Tulsa office
jokes.punchline: Boss's enthusiastic assumption he'll love famously boring state
jokes.explanation: Standard 'unwanted assignment' setup. The Oklahoma reference works but isn't particularly clever.

dialogue.speaker: Boss
dialogue.line: You're going to love Tulsa! It's got everything — a river, a couple of chains restaurants, and some really great dust storms.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, what's not to love?

jokes.text: Try this one. 'Go back in time and listen to Phoebe.'
jokes.setup: Monica reading baby care suggestions from book
jokes.punchline: Phoebe suggests time travel to follow her earlier advice about not waking baby
jokes.explanation: Good callback that acknowledges Phoebe was right. The time travel framing adds humor to 'I told you so.'

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: The book says here that you shouldn't wake the baby.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Well, maybe if you had a time machine, you could go back and not wake the baby in the first place!

jokes.text: No. Just stopped to throw up a little bit.
jokes.setup: Monica thinks rapid baby swinging worked to stop crying
jokes.punchline: Baby only paused to vomit from motion sickness
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke. Sets up false hope then deflates it with gross-out reality.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: It worked! The rapid swinging stopped the baby from crying!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Monica, the baby didn't stop crying because of the swinging. The baby paused to vomit from motion sickness.

jokes.text: She, Rach. Not it. She.
jokes.setup: Rachel refers to crying baby as 'it'
jokes.punchline: Monica corrects the dehumanizing language
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate correction from Monica. Minor but shows her nurturing instincts vs Rachel's frustration.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh, it's crying again.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: It? It's a baby, Rachel. A baby is a 'he' or a 'she,' not an 'it.'

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Well, I don't know if it's a boy or a girl!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Then ask! Don't just refer to a human being as 'it.'

jokes.text: All right, this just proves no good can come from having sex with Ross!
jokes.setup: Escalating frustration with crying baby situation
jokes.punchline: Monica blames the entire baby on Ross as sexual partner
jokes.explanation: Monica's exasperation leads to blaming Ross entirely. Dark comedy of treating baby as consequence of poor sexual choices.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I can't believe this baby won't stop crying! Ross, this is all your fault!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: My fault? How is this my fault?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, if you were a better... you know... we wouldn't have had this baby in the first place!

jokes.text: l can't even believe she slept with you in the first place.
jokes.setup: Gunther commenting on Ross and Rachel having a baby
jokes.punchline: His long-standing crush makes him openly insult Ross
jokes.explanation: Classic Gunther jealousy moment. Consistent with his character's unrequited love for Rachel.

dialogue.speaker: Gunther
dialogue.line: A baby? Ross and Rachel are having a baby? Well, I hope the kid has Rachel's looks and Ross's... well, actually, I hope it just has Rachel's looks. Poor kid doesn't stand a chance with those Geller genes.

jokes.text: You mean, like....
jokes.setup: Gunther's brutal comment about Ross and Rachel sleeping together
jokes.punchline: Ross's stunned, speechless reaction to the insult
jokes.explanation: Ross's bewildered non-response to Gunther's insult. Simple reaction beat that works in context.

dialogue.speaker: Gunther
dialogue.line: She'll never be with you. She doesn't even know you exist.

jokes.text: When people do this: l don't really know what that means.
jokes.setup: Ross making air quotes while talking
jokes.punchline: Joey admits he doesn't understand air quotes gesture
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey confusion about basic cultural gesture. Shows his endearing obliviousness to common concepts.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey, what are you doing with your hands?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: They're air quotes.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh. I thought you were trying to catch something invisible.

jokes.text: Not using it right, Joe.
jokes.setup: Joey attempts to use air quotes after Ross explains them
jokes.punchline: Ross corrects Joey's immediate misuse of the gesture
jokes.explanation: Quick payoff to Joey's air quotes confusion. Shows he still doesn't get it despite explanation.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: So you just do this? *makes air quotes gesture incorrectly*

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: No, no, no! Joey, that's not how you do air quotes.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: What? I'm doing it exactly like you showed me!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: You have to use two fingers on each hand, and you actually have to make the quote mark motion.

jokes.text: You ducked! l'm sorry! lt was a reflex!
jokes.setup: Joey kept insisting Ross should hit him to feel better
jokes.punchline: When Ross finally swings, Joey instinctively ducks, defeating the purpose
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy payoff. Joey's natural reflexes undermine his 'let me help' gesture, causing more problems.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Okay, you know what? Fine! I'll hit you!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah? Come on!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: *swings*

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: *ducks* Whoa, whoa, whoa!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What are you doing?!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: What? I'm sorry, it's instinct!

jokes.text: When a fist comes at your face, you duck. Look.
jokes.setup: Joey explaining why he ducked Ross's punch
jokes.punchline: Demonstrates by making Ross duck, then presumably hitting him
jokes.explanation: Escalates the physical comedy by having Joey accidentally hit Ross while demonstrating reflexes.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: You gotta do it like this.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Duck!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Duck!

jokes.text: l told you you shouldn't have let her watch Terms of Endearment.
jokes.setup: Baby Emma continues crying inconsolably
jokes.punchline: Chandler blames exposure to famously sad movie for baby's distress
jokes.explanation: Clever absurdist explanation for baby's crying. Good Chandler-style pop culture reference with logical twist.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Maybe she saw The Notebook.

jokes.text: l knew l should've married Chandler.
jokes.setup: Monica says baby discussion is 'husband-and-wife' thing, excluding Phoebe
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's regret about not marrying Chandler for inclusion privileges
jokes.explanation: Phoebe's practical approach to marriage for social access. Absurd logic delivered matter-of-factly.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh, so that's what I've been missing! I could've married Chandler and been part of all the husband-and-wife stuff!

jokes.text: You know how people say that Tulsa... is the Paris of Oklahoma?
jokes.setup: Monica trying to find positive spin on moving to Tulsa
jokes.punchline: Extremely weak comparison that makes Tulsa sound worse
jokes.explanation: Monica's desperate attempt at optimism backfires. The pause before 'Oklahoma' makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Okay, okay. Tulsa is just like New York... if New York were flat, had no Broadway, and everyone there drove pickup trucks.

jokes.text: What? Who says that? People who have never been to Paris?
jokes.setup: Monica's weak Tulsa-Paris comparison
jokes.punchline: Chandler's logical deconstruction of the absurd comparison
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler response - logically dismantling Monica's desperate optimism with sarcastic precision.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Okay, Monica, let me get this straight. You're comparing Tulsa to Paris? That's like comparing a gas station bathroom to the Louvre. One has a toilet that probably works half the time, and the other is in France.

jokes.text: Are you trying to tell me we're moving to Oklahoma or that you're gay?
jokes.setup: Chandler mentions Oklahoma musical songs to cheer Monica up
jokes.punchline: Monica questions whether he's breaking news about move or coming out
jokes.explanation: Monica's confusion about Chandler's musical theater enthusiasm. Plays on stereotypes about theater appreciation.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Wait, are you telling me you're moving to Oklahoma, or are you coming out?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I'm trying to cheer you up!

jokes.text: No. But they are going to lease us a Ford Focus.
jokes.setup: Monica asks if they at least offered huge raise for Tulsa move
jokes.punchline: Pathetically modest car lease as only compensation
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between expectation of big raise and reality of modest car lease. Chandler's deadpan delivery sells it.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: They offered me a pathetically modest car lease as my only compensation.

jokes.text: She misunderstood. She thought she was moving to Tulsa.
jokes.setup: Chandler asks what's wrong with baby Emma
jokes.punchline: Monica blames baby's crying on overhearing Tulsa conversation
jokes.explanation: Creative explanation for baby's distress. Treats newborn like she has adult concerns about relocation.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: What's wrong with Emma? Why is she crying?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: She overheard us talking about Tulsa.

jokes.text: lrony. Not a fan? All right.
jokes.setup: Chandler's ironic joke about falling asleep falls flat with boss
jokes.punchline: Meta-commentary on his failed attempt at humor
jokes.explanation: Self-aware comedy about failed comedy. Chandler acknowledging his humor doesn't land with authority figures.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I'm not sleeping, I'm just resting my eyes. It's like meditation, but with more drool.

dialogue.speaker: Boss
dialogue.line: That's not funny, Bing.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, I can see that by the complete lack of laughter in the room. It's like a silent film, except without the artistic merit.

jokes.text: See me, l love Tulsa. Tulsa is heaven. Tulsa is ltaly.
jokes.setup: Chandler trying to convince boss not to send him to Tulsa
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd praise for place he's desperately trying to avoid
jokes.explanation: Chandler's panicked reverse psychology. The escalating comparisons show his desperation clearly.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Sir, I really don't want to go to Tulsa. It's a terrible place. Nothing good ever happens in Tulsa.

dialogue.speaker: Boss
dialogue.line: What are you talking about? Tulsa is wonderful! It's got great restaurants, beautiful parks...

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yes, but—

dialogue.speaker: Boss
dialogue.line: And the people there are incredibly friendly. World-class museums. A thriving arts scene.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I understand, but I really can't—

dialogue.speaker: Boss
dialogue.line: The sunsets are breathtaking. It's practically the Paris of Oklahoma.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Please, I—

dialogue.speaker: Boss
dialogue.line: I'm thinking of moving there myself. It's basically a utopia. Shangri-La. The Garden of Eden before the fall.

jokes.text: Why would l when your name is Elaine?
jokes.setup: Chandler asks boss if he can call her Carol
jokes.punchline: Reveals he doesn't know his boss's actual name
jokes.explanation: Classic workplace awkwardness. Chandler's incompetence at office politics costs him.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Can I call you Carol?

dialogue.speaker: Boss
dialogue.line: My name is Carol.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh, thank God. I had no idea what your name was.

jokes.text: That's a picture of your daughter, isn't it? Well, she's lovely. l like a girl with a strong jaw.
jokes.setup: Chandler calls boss's daughter photo a 'strapping son'
jokes.punchline: Doubles down with backhanded compliment about masculine features
jokes.explanation: Escalates Chandler's workplace disasters. The 'strong jaw' comment makes the gender mistake worse.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, she has very strong, masculine features. Very angular. She could be a, uh, diesel mechanic or a lumberjack.

jokes.text: Why, does it look like l'm having trouble with my misshapen claw?
jokes.setup: Joey offers to help Ross fill out forms with broken thumb
jokes.punchline: Ross sarcastically describes his injury in disturbing terms
jokes.explanation: Ross's bitter self-pity expressed through grotesque imagery. Good character moment of wounded pride.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I can help you fill out those forms with your broken thumb.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Oh sure, because that's exactly what I need right now - someone touching my injury while I'm vulnerable and in pain.

jokes.text: You know, like Rossell. Or Rosstrophor.
jokes.setup: Joey asking what Ross is short for
jokes.punchline: Bizarre made-up long versions of simple name
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey logic fail. The made-up names 'Rossell' and 'Rosstrophor' are absurd but feel authentically Joey.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: What is Ross short for?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Ross.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: No, I mean what's it short for? Like how Bob is short for Robert.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Oh. Um, it's just Ross.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Okay, what about... Roseph? Or Rosario?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: No, it's just Ross.

jokes.text: May...tember?
jokes.setup: Joey trying to remember Ross's birthday month
jokes.punchline: Creates nonsense month combining May and September
jokes.explanation: Joey's brain creating hybrid month when confused. Perfect character moment showing his scrambled thinking process.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: When's Ross's birthday again?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: May.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Right, right, May... or was it September?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: It's May, Joe.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh yeah, yeah, May-tember. I got it now.

jokes.text: Actually, l'm a paleon-- Dinosaurs is fine.
jokes.setup: Joey writes 'Dinosaurs' as Ross's occupation
jokes.punchline: Ross starts to correct then gives up on proper terminology
jokes.explanation: Ross's defeated acceptance of Joey's simplification. Character development showing him learning to pick his battles.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait. I'm not a dinosaur. I'm a paleontologist. A paleontologist studies dinosaurs. There's a big difference.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey, to me, you're a dinosaur.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I'm a... you know what? Never mind.

jokes.text: That's why they call them plans. Hello?
jokes.setup: Ross explaining importance of sticking to plans
jokes.punchline: Phone rings mid-philosophy, undermining his serious moment
jokes.explanation: Timing gag where phone interrupts Ross's attempt at profundity. Simple but effective deflation.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: That's why it's so important to stick to your plans. You can't just abandon your commitments because something else comes along. It's about discipline and—

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Hold on, my phone's ringing.

jokes.text: No? Really? Misery really does love company?
jokes.setup: Friends won't let Chandler leave during baby crying crisis
jokes.punchline: References famous saying about shared suffering
jokes.explanation: Chandler observing the literal truth of the misery loves company cliché. Mild but appropriate.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: You know what they say - misery loves company.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, misery just moved in with us.

jokes.text: Yeah, l don't think dressing provocatively is going to help me here!
jokes.setup: Monica says Rachel figured out her first job, so she'll figure out mothering
jokes.punchline: Rachel references using sex appeal at work won't work on baby
jokes.explanation: Self-aware callback to Rachel's past workplace behavior. Good character insight about her professional methods.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Well, I mean, I figured out the whole work thing, so how hard can mothering be?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Rachel, you can't just use your sex appeal on a baby.

jokes.text: You must be a fireball in bed!
jokes.setup: Monica successfully gets baby to sleep with repetitive 'baby baby baby' chanting
jokes.punchline: Rachel connects Monica's rhythmic skills to sexual prowess
jokes.explanation: Crude but effective connection between baby-calming rhythm and sexual skill. Gets the job done.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God, Monica. With skills like that, no wonder Chandler's always so happy.

jokes.text: l can hear the voices in my head again. l'm kidding.
jokes.setup: Monica expressing relief at baby finally being quiet
jokes.punchline: Jokes about hearing voices, then quickly clarifies it's a joke
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about mental state, quickly defused with clarification. Captures exhaustion-induced delirium.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh thank god, the baby's finally quiet.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I can actually hear myself think again. Although, to be honest, all I hear are voices telling me I'm doing everything wrong.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I'm kidding! I'm kidding. Those are just my mother.

jokes.text: Rachel? Let's give it six to eight months.
jokes.setup: Rachel declares Monica can never leave apartment due to baby-calming skills
jokes.punchline: Monica negotiates a more reasonable time commitment
jokes.explanation: Monica's practical approach to Rachel's desperate demands. Reasonable compromise to unreasonable request.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Monica, you can never leave this apartment. Your baby-calming skills are too valuable.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What? Rachel, I have a life!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Not anymore.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Okay, how about I'm here Monday through Thursday, and weekends I'm free?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Deal.

jokes.text: What?
jokes.setup: Chandler announces they're moving to Tulsa in whispered voice
jokes.punchline: Monica's loud 'What?' threatens to wake baby they just got to sleep
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective setup for Monica's shock potentially undoing all their baby-calming work.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: We're moving to Tulsa.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Shh!

jokes.text: lf you think this is bad, you should see him.
jokes.setup: Phoebe asking what happened to Ross's injured hand
jokes.punchline: Ross implies Joey looks even worse than his obvious injury
jokes.explanation: Standard 'you should see the other guy' joke with callback to earlier physical comedy sequence.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: What happened to your hand?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Oh, I broke it.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh my God! How?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Well, you should see the other guy.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey!

jokes.text: Emma: not easy.
jokes.setup: Rachel listing all the exhausting aspects of new motherhood
jokes.punchline: Massive understatement summarizing the obvious difficulty
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy in understatement. Two words sum up the entire episode's parenting struggles.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: This is hard.

jokes.text: Okay, what would you do if l did this?
jokes.setup: Joey explaining his ducking was natural instinct
jokes.punchline: Presumably demonstrates by making threatening gesture at Ross
jokes.explanation: Continues the physical comedy thread from earlier. Sets up final gag but isn't particularly strong on its own.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: It's a natural instinct!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: *makes threatening gesture at Ross*

tmdb_overview: Chandler, having trouble getting enough sleep at home, falls asleep in a meeting and accidentally agrees to run his company's Tulsa office. Emma won't stop crying after Rachel interrupts her nap; Monica and Phoebe try to help get her back to sleep. Meanwhile, Ross is still mad at Joey, and breaks his finger trying to punch him.
insight: Post-birth comedy struggles: 66 score, 2.7 jokes/min signals Season 9 slump begins.
